sentences.sentence: When the US explorer and academic Hiram Bingham arrived in South America in 1911, he was ready for what was to be the greatest achievement of his life: the exploration of the remote hinterland to the west of Cusco, the old capital of the Inca empire in the Andes mountains of Peru.
sentences.translation: 1911年，美国探险家兼学者海勒姆·宾厄姆抵达南美时，他已经准备迎接后来成为他一生最大成就的事情：探索库斯科以西偏远的内陆地区；库斯科是秘鲁安第斯山脉中印加帝国的旧都。

structure.text: When the US explorer and academic Hiram Bingham arrived in South America in 1911
he
was ready for what was to be the greatest achievement of his life
what was to be the greatest achievement of his life
the exploration of the remote hinterland to the west of Cusco, the old capital of the Inca empire in the Andes mountains of Peru
the old capital of the Inca empire in the Andes mountains of Peru

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，先交代宾厄姆抵达南美的时间和身份。
what was to be 表示“后来注定会成为”，带有回顾历史时的预示意味。
冒号后的 the exploration 是对前面 greatest achievement 的具体解释。
the old capital of the Inca empire 是 Cusco 的同位语，继续补充其历史身份。

sentences.sentence: His goal was to locate the remains of a city called Vitcos, the last capital of the Inca civilisation.
sentences.translation: 他的目标是找到一座名叫维特科斯的城市遗迹，那是印加文明最后的都城。

structure.text: His goal
was to locate the remains of a city called Vitcos
to locate the remains of a city called Vitcos
called Vitcos
the last capital of the Inca civilisation

difficult_points: to locate 作表语，说明 goal 的具体内容。
called Vitcos 是过去分词短语，修饰 a city。
逗号后的 the last capital 是同位语，说明 Vitcos 的历史地位。

sentences.sentence: Cusco lies on a high plateau at an elevation of more than 3,000 metres, and Bingham's plan was to descend from this plateau along the valley of the Urubamba river, which takes a circuitous route down to the Amazon and passes through an area of dramatic canyons and mountain ranges.
sentences.translation: 库斯科位于一片海拔三千多米的高原上，而宾厄姆的计划是从这片高原沿乌鲁班巴河谷下降；这条河谷迂回通向亚马孙，并穿过一片峡谷和山脉壮观的地区。

structure.text: Cusco
lies on a high plateau at an elevation of more than 3,000 metres
and Bingham's plan was to descend from this plateau along the valley of the Urubamba river
to descend from this plateau along the valley of the Urubamba river
which takes a circuitous route down to the Amazon and passes through an area of dramatic canyons and mountain ranges

difficult_points: 句子由 and 连接两个并列分句，先说明库斯科位置，再说明宾厄姆的路线计划。
at an elevation of more than 3,000 metres 是介词短语，补充高原的海拔。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 the valley of the Urubamba river。
takes 和 passes 是 which 从句中的并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: When Bingham and his team set off down the Urubamba in late July, they had an advantage over travellers who had preceded them: a track had recently been blasted down the valley canyon to enable rubber to be brought up by mules from the jungle.
sentences.translation: 七月底，宾厄姆和他的团队沿乌鲁班巴河出发时，他们比此前到过那里的人有一个优势：不久前，人们在河谷峡谷中炸开了一条小路，以便用骡子把橡胶从丛林中运上来。

structure.text: When Bingham and his team set off down the Urubamba in late July
they
had an advantage over travellers who had preceded them
who had preceded them
a track had recently been blasted down the valley canyon
to enable rubber to be brought up by mules from the jungle

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，主句是 they had an advantage over travellers。
who had preceded them 修饰 travellers，说明这些旅行者在宾厄姆之前到过那里。
冒号后的 a track had recently been blasted 解释 advantage 的具体内容。
to enable rubber to be brought up 表目的，其中 rubber 是被运送的对象。

sentences.sentence: Almost all previous travellers had left the river at Ollantaytambo and taken a high pass across the mountains to rejoin the river lower down, thereby cutting a substantial corner, but also therefore never passing through the area around Machu Picchu.
sentences.translation: 几乎所有先前的旅行者都在奥扬泰坦博离开河流，翻过山间高处的隘口，在下游重新回到河边，这样大幅抄了近路；但也正因如此，他们从未经过马丘比丘周围的地区。

structure.text: Almost all previous travellers
had left the river at Ollantaytambo and taken a high pass across the mountains
to rejoin the river lower down
thereby cutting a substantial corner
but also therefore never passing through the area around Machu Picchu

difficult_points: had left 和 taken 是过去完成时的并列谓语，表示发生在宾厄姆旅程之前的路线选择。
to rejoin the river lower down 表示翻山之后重新回到下游河边的目的。
thereby cutting a substantial corner 说明前面路线选择造成的结果，即抄了近路。
but also therefore never passing through 与 thereby cutting 并列，指出这种捷径也让他们错过了马丘比丘区域。

sentences.sentence: On 24 July they were a few days into their descent of the valley.
sentences.translation: 到7月24日，他们已经沿山谷下行了几天。

structure.text: On 24 July
they
were a few days into their descent of the valley

difficult_points: On 24 July 是时间状语，定位叙事日期。
a few days into 表示某一过程已经进行了几天，而不是距离开始还有几天。
their descent of the valley 指他们沿山谷向下行进的过程。

sentences.sentence: The day began slowly, with Bingham trying to arrange sufficient mules for the next stage of the trek.
sentences.translation: 这一天开始得很缓慢，宾厄姆忙着为下一段徒步行程安排足够的骡子。

structure.text: The day
began slowly
with Bingham trying to arrange sufficient mules for the next stage of the trek
trying to arrange sufficient mules
for the next stage of the trek

difficult_points: with Bingham trying... 是 with 复合结构，补充说明当天进展缓慢的背景。
trying to arrange 中现在分词 trying 描述宾厄姆当时正在做的事。
for the next stage of the trek 表示这些骡子要用于下一段行程。

sentences.sentence: His companions showed no interest in accompanying him up the nearby hill to see some ruins that a local farmer, Melchor Arteaga, had told them about the night before.
sentences.translation: 他的同伴们毫无兴趣陪他爬上附近的小山，去看一些遗迹；这些遗迹是一位当地农民梅尔乔·阿特亚加前一晚告诉他们的。

structure.text: His companions
showed no interest in accompanying him up the nearby hill
in accompanying him up the nearby hill
to see some ruins
that a local farmer, Melchor Arteaga, had told them about the night before
Melchor Arteaga

difficult_points: showed no interest in 后接动名词 accompanying，表示“不感兴趣做某事”。
to see some ruins 表目的，说明上山的原因。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 some ruins。
Melchor Arteaga 是 a local farmer 的同位语，提供农民姓名。

sentences.sentence: The morning was dull and damp, and Bingham also seems to have been less than keen on the prospect of climbing the hill.
sentences.translation: 那天早晨阴沉潮湿，宾厄姆似乎也对爬这座山的前景不太热衷。

structure.text: The morning
was dull and damp
and Bingham also seems to have been less than keen on the prospect of climbing the hill
to have been less than keen on the prospect of climbing the hill
of climbing the hill

difficult_points: and 连接天气情况和宾厄姆的态度两个分句。
seems to have been 使用完成式不定式，表示从叙述者角度推断他当时已经处于这种态度。
less than keen 是委婉说法，意思是并不热衷。
the prospect of climbing the hill 中 of 后接动名词 climbing。

sentences.sentence: In his book Lost City of the Incas, he relates that he made the ascent without having the least expectation that he would find anything at the top.
sentences.translation: 在他的《失落的印加城市》一书中，他讲述说，自己爬上去时完全没有预期会在山顶发现任何东西。

structure.text: In his book Lost City of the Incas
he
relates that he made the ascent without having the least expectation
that he made the ascent without having the least expectation
without having the least expectation
that he would find anything at the top

difficult_points: relates 后接 that 引导的宾语从句，说明书中叙述的内容。
without having the least expectation 表示“完全没有预期”，least 加强否定意味。
第二个 that 从句解释 expectation 的具体内容，即会在山顶发现东西。
made the ascent 中 ascent 指上山、攀登的过程。

sentences.sentence: Bingham writes about the approach in vivid style in his book.
sentences.translation: 宾厄姆在书中以生动的笔调描写了接近遗址的过程。

structure.text: Bingham
writes about the approach
in vivid style
in his book

difficult_points: the approach 指接近马丘比丘遗址的过程，而不是抽象的“方法”。
两个 in 短语分别表示写作风格和出现的位置。
vivid style 强调叙述具有强烈的画面感。

sentences.sentence: First, as he climbs up the hill, he describes the ever-present possibility of deadly snakes, 'capable of making considerable springs when in pursuit of their prey'; not that he sees any.
sentences.translation: 首先，当他爬上山时，他描写了随时可能出现的致命蛇类——它们“在追捕猎物时能够大幅跃起”；不过他并没有真的看到任何蛇。

structure.text: as he climbs up the hill
he
describes the ever-present possibility of deadly snakes
'capable of making considerable springs when in pursuit of their prey'
when in pursuit of their prey
not that he sees any

difficult_points: as 引导时间状语从句，说明描写发生在他爬山时。
ever-present possibility 表示危险似乎一直存在，但并不等于他真的遇到了蛇。
引号中的 capable of... 补充 deadly snakes 的能力。
not that he sees any 是补充性转折，指出他其实没有看到蛇。

sentences.sentence: Then there's a sense of mounting discovery as he comes across great sweeps of terraces, then a mausoleum, followed by monumental staircases and, finally, the grand ceremonial buildings of Machu Picchu.
sentences.translation: 接着，随着他先后遇到大片梯田、一座陵墓、宏伟的阶梯，最后看到马丘比丘宏大的祭祀建筑，一种发现不断推进的感觉逐渐增强。

structure.text: Then there's a sense of mounting discovery
as he comes across great sweeps of terraces
then a mausoleum
followed by monumental staircases
and, finally, the grand ceremonial buildings of Machu Picchu

difficult_points: there's a sense of... 是存在句，强调叙事中出现了一种感觉。
mounting discovery 中 mounting 表示逐渐增强、逐步累积。
as 引导时间状语从句，说明这种感觉随着他看到不同遗迹而出现。
then、followed by 和 finally 构成递进式列举，制造逐步发现的效果。

sentences.sentence: 'It seemed like an unbelievable dream ... the sight held me spellbound ...' he wrote.
sentences.translation: 他写道：“那看起来像一个难以置信的梦……眼前的景象让我着了迷……”

structure.text: 'It seemed like an unbelievable dream ... the sight held me spellbound ...'
It
seemed like an unbelievable dream
the sight held me spellbound
he wrote

difficult_points: 引号内是宾厄姆原文式的直接引语，he wrote 是后置的报道语。
seemed like 表示主观感受，不是在陈述事实上的“梦”。
held me spellbound 表示景象让他完全入迷，held 在这里不是字面“拿住”。

sentences.sentence: We should remember, however, that Lost City of the Incas is a work of hindsight, not written until 1948, many years after his journey.
sentences.translation: 然而，我们应该记住，《失落的印加城市》是一部事后回顾之作，直到1948年才写成，距离他的旅程已经过去许多年。

structure.text: We
should remember
that Lost City of the Incas is a work of hindsight
not written until 1948
many years after his journey

difficult_points: however 插入主句中，提示作者要对上一段的戏剧性叙述作出限定。
that 引导 remember 的宾语从句。
a work of hindsight 表示这本书是事后回顾写成的，不是当时即时记录。
not written until 1948 是过去分词短语，补充说明这本书的写作时间。

sentences.sentence: His journal entries of the time reveal a much more gradual appreciation of his achievement.
sentences.translation: 他当时的日记条目显示，他对自己成就的认识是逐渐加深的。

structure.text: His journal entries of the time
reveal a much more gradual appreciation of his achievement
of the time
of his achievement

difficult_points: of the time 修饰 journal entries，指旅程当时写下的记录。
reveal 表示这些记录间接显示出事实，而不是直接宣称。
a much more gradual appreciation 指他并非立刻意识到重大发现，而是逐步认识到其价值。

sentences.sentence: He spent the afternoon at the ruins noting down the dimensions of some of the buildings, then descended and rejoined his companions, to whom he seems to have said little about his discovery.
sentences.translation: 他整个下午都在遗址处记录一些建筑的尺寸，随后下山并重新与同伴会合；对于自己的发现，他似乎几乎没有向他们说起。

structure.text: He
spent the afternoon at the ruins noting down the dimensions of some of the buildings
noting down the dimensions of some of the buildings
then descended and rejoined his companions
to whom he seems to have said little about his discovery

difficult_points: spent the afternoon ... noting down 使用 spend time doing 结构，说明下午的主要活动。
then descended and rejoined 是后续动作，与 spent 并列推进叙事。
to whom 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 his companions。
seems to have said little 表示根据记录推断他当时说得很少。

sentences.sentence: At this stage, Bingham didn't realise the extent or the importance of the site, nor did he realise what use he could make of the discovery.
sentences.translation: 在这个阶段，宾厄姆还没有意识到这个遗址的规模或重要性，也没有意识到自己可以如何利用这一发现。

structure.text: At this stage
Bingham
didn't realise the extent or the importance of the site
nor did he realise what use he could make of the discovery
what use he could make of the discovery

difficult_points: At this stage 限定当时的认知水平，而不是他后来的看法。
the extent or the importance 连接两个他尚未意识到的方面：规模和重要性。
nor did he realise 是否定并列分句，nor 位于句首引发倒装。
what use he could make of the discovery 是 realise 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: However, soon after returning it occurred to him that he could make a name for himself from this discovery.
sentences.translation: 然而，回来后不久，他想到自己可以凭借这一发现成名。

structure.text: soon after returning
it
occurred to him
that he could make a name for himself from this discovery
make a name for himself

difficult_points: However 表示与上一段“当时未意识到重要性”形成转折。
it occurred to him that... 是固定句式，it 为形式主语，真正内容在 that 从句中。
make a name for himself 表示为自己赢得名声。
from this discovery 表示成名所凭借的来源。

sentences.sentence: When he came to write the National Geographic magazine article that broke the story to the world in April 1913, he knew he had to produce a big idea.
sentences.translation: 当他开始撰写那篇于1913年4月把这一故事公之于世的《国家地理》杂志文章时，他知道自己必须提出一个宏大的想法。

structure.text: When he came to write the National Geographic magazine article that broke the story to the world in April 1913
that broke the story to the world in April 1913
he
knew he had to produce a big idea
he had to produce a big idea

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，说明他意识到需要“大想法”的时间背景。
that broke the story to the world 修饰 article，说明这篇文章把消息传播给世界。
knew 后省略了宾语从句引导词 that。
had to produce 表示出于发表和传播需要，他必须提出一个有吸引力的解释。

expressions.text: break the story to the world

sentences.sentence: He wondered whether it could have been the birthplace of the very first Inca, Manco the Great, and whether it could also have been what chroniclers described as 'the last city of the Incas'.
sentences.translation: 他揣测那里是否可能是第一位印加人曼科大帝的出生地，也是否可能就是编年史作者所描述的“印加人的最后城市”。

structure.text: He
wondered whether it could have been the birthplace of the very first Inca
whether it could have been the birthplace of the very first Inca
Manco the Great
and whether it could also have been what chroniclers described as 'the last city of the Incas'
what chroniclers described as 'the last city of the Incas'

difficult_points: wondered 后接两个由 whether 引导的宾语从句，表示两个猜想。
could have been 表示对过去身份的推测，而不是确定事实。
Manco the Great 是 the very first Inca 的同位语。
what chroniclers described as... 是名词性从句，作第二个 been 的表语。

sentences.sentence: This term refers to Vilcabamba, the settlement where the Incas had fled from Spanish invaders in the 1530s.
sentences.translation: 这个说法指的是维尔卡班巴，即印加人在16世纪30年代为躲避西班牙入侵者而逃去的那个定居点。

structure.text: This term
refers to Vilcabamba
the settlement where the Incas had fled from Spanish invaders in the 1530s
where the Incas had fled from Spanish invaders in the 1530s

difficult_points: This term 回指上一句中的 'the last city of the Incas'。
refers to 表示这个术语所指代的对象。
the settlement 是 Vilcabamba 的同位语。
where 引导定语从句，说明这个定居点与印加人逃避西班牙入侵者有关。

sentences.sentence: Bingham made desperate attempts to prove this belief for nearly 40 years.
sentences.translation: 近40年来，宾厄姆一直竭力试图证明这一信念。

structure.text: Bingham
made desperate attempts to prove this belief
to prove this belief
for nearly 40 years

difficult_points: made desperate attempts to do 表示努力程度很强，带有执着甚至急切的意味。
this belief 指前文关于马丘比丘就是“印加人的最后城市”等判断。
for nearly 40 years 强调他坚持证明这一观点的时间之长。

sentences.sentence: Sadly, his vision of the site as both the beginning and end of the Inca civilisation, while a magnificent one, is inaccurate.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，他把这个遗址视为印加文明起点和终点的构想虽然宏伟，却并不准确。

structure.text: his vision of the site as both the beginning and end of the Inca civilisation
of the site as both the beginning and end of the Inca civilisation
while a magnificent one
is inaccurate

difficult_points: 主语是 his vision of the site as...，而不是 the site 本身。
as both the beginning and end of the Inca civilisation 说明他赋予遗址的双重象征意义。
while a magnificent one 是让步性插入语，承认构想宏伟但不改变结论。
is inaccurate 是句子核心判断，否定宾厄姆的解释。

expressions.text: both the beginning and end of

expressions.meaning_zh: 虽然是一个宏伟的构想

sentences.sentence: We now know that Vilcabamba actually lies 65 kilometres away in the depths of the jungle.
sentences.translation: 现在我们知道，维尔卡班巴实际上位于65公里外的丛林深处。

structure.text: We
now know
that Vilcabamba actually lies 65 kilometres away in the depths of the jungle
in the depths of the jungle

difficult_points: that 引导 know 的宾语从句，给出后来考证的事实。
actually 强调事实与宾厄姆的判断相反。
65 kilometres away 说明距离马丘比丘相当远。
in the depths of the jungle 表示在丛林深处，进一步说明位置偏远。

sentences.sentence: One question that has perplexed visitors, historians and archaeologists alike ever since Bingham, is why the site seems to have been abandoned before the Spanish Conquest.
sentences.translation: 自宾厄姆以来，一个同样困扰游客、历史学家和考古学家的问题是：为什么这个遗址似乎在西班牙征服之前就已经被遗弃。

structure.text: One question that has perplexed visitors, historians and archaeologists alike ever since Bingham
that has perplexed visitors, historians and archaeologists alike ever since Bingham
is
why the site seems to have been abandoned before the Spanish Conquest
to have been abandoned before the Spanish Conquest

difficult_points: that has perplexed... 是定语从句，修饰 One question。
alike 表示游客、历史学家和考古学家都同样被这个问题困扰。
why 引导表语从句，说明这个问题的具体内容。
seems to have been abandoned 使用完成式被动不定式，表示遗址似乎在西班牙征服前就已被遗弃。

sentences.sentence: There are no references to it by any of the Spanish chroniclers - and if they had known of its existence so close to Cusco they would certainly have come in search of gold.
sentences.translation: 任何西班牙编年史作者都没有提到它；而且如果他们知道它就在离库斯科这么近的地方，他们肯定会前来寻找黄金。

structure.text: There are no references to it by any of the Spanish chroniclers
and if they had known of its existence so close to Cusco they would certainly have come in search of gold
if they had known of its existence so close to Cusco
they would certainly have come in search of gold
in search of gold

difficult_points: There are no references to it 表示文献中没有提到该遗址，it 指 Machu Picchu。
破折号后的 and 引出作者的推理。
if they had known... they would have come... 是对过去事实相反的虚拟条件句。
in search of gold 表示他们前来的目的。

sentences.sentence: An idea which has gained wide acceptance over the past few years is that Machu Picchu was a moya, a country estate built by an Inca emperor to escape the cold winters of Cusco, where the elite could enjoy monumental architecture and spectacular views.
sentences.translation: 过去几年里，一个已获得广泛接受的观点是：马丘比丘是一处 moya，即由一位印加皇帝建造、用来逃离库斯科寒冷冬季的乡间庄园，在那里精英阶层可以享受宏伟的建筑和壮丽景色。

structure.text: An idea which has gained wide acceptance over the past few years
which has gained wide acceptance over the past few years
is
that Machu Picchu was a moya
a country estate built by an Inca emperor to escape the cold winters of Cusco
where the elite could enjoy monumental architecture and spectacular views

difficult_points: which has gained wide acceptance 修饰 An idea，说明这个观点近年被广泛接受。
that Machu Picchu was a moya 是表语从句，说明 idea 的具体内容。
a country estate 是 moya 的同位解释，built by... 是过去分词短语修饰 estate。
where 引导定语从句，说明精英在这处庄园能享受的环境。

sentences.sentence: Furthermore, the particular architecture of Machu Picchu suggests that it was constructed at the time of the greatest of all the Incas, the emperor Pachacuti (c. 1438-71).
sentences.translation: 此外，马丘比丘独特的建筑风格表明，它建于所有印加统治者中最伟大的一位——帕查库蒂皇帝（约1438—1471年）在位时期。

structure.text: the particular architecture of Machu Picchu
suggests that it was constructed at the time of the greatest of all the Incas
that it was constructed at the time of the greatest of all the Incas
at the time of the greatest of all the Incas
the emperor Pachacuti (c. 1438-71)

difficult_points: Furthermore 表示在前一句关于用途的解释之外，继续增加建筑年代方面的证据。
suggests 后接 that 宾语从句，说明建筑特征所指向的结论。
was constructed 是被动语态，强调遗址被建造的时间。
the emperor Pachacuti 是 the greatest of all the Incas 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: By custom, Pachacuti's descendants built other similar estates for their own use, and so Machu Picchu would have been abandoned after his death, some 50 years before the Spanish Conquest.
sentences.translation: 按照惯例，帕查库蒂的后代会为自己修建其他类似的庄园，因此马丘比丘很可能在他去世后就被遗弃了，时间大约比西班牙征服早50年。

structure.text: By custom
Pachacuti's descendants
built other similar estates for their own use
and so Machu Picchu would have been abandoned after his death
after his death
some 50 years before the Spanish Conquest

difficult_points: By custom 表示依据当时惯例来推断后续发展。
and so 引出结果：后代另建庄园，因此马丘比丘会被弃置。
would have been abandoned 是对过去情况的推断，使用被动完成形式。
some 50 years before the Spanish Conquest 是时间补充，说明遗弃发生在西班牙征服前约50年。
